seo.title: Building Customer Support with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with OpenAI Assistants API enables powerful RAG-based conversations with CRM integration, knowledge base retrieval, and automated ticket creation. However, the built-in code interpreter, file search, and function calling introduce significant risks like unsafe executions, irrelevant retrievals, and hallucinated tool calls. Vex provides essential verification guardrails to ensure response accuracy, PII safety, tone consistency, and policy compliance in high-risk customer interactions.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with a customer query routed to an OpenAI Assistant configured with a custom GPT model fine-tuned for support tone. The assistant accesses a knowledge base via file search for product docs and FAQs, integrates with CRM via function calling for order lookup and ticket creation, and uses code interpreter for data analysis like refund calculations. Threads maintain conversation context across interactions, with webhooks triggering on run completion to verify outputs before delivery to customers.

Key components include: Assistant configuration with tools (file_search, code_interpreter, function calling), persistent threads for context, vector stores for RAG, and CRM APIs (e.g., Zendesk, Salesforce) for actions. Run completion webhooks enable Vex verification of response accuracy, PII filtering, and escalation logic before final customer response.

This setup scales to thousands of concurrent support threads but requires guardrails to prevent context bloat, unsafe code execution, and compliance violations.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐
│   Customer      │───▶│ OpenAI Assistants │
│   Query         │    │ + Thread Mgmt    │
└─────────────────┘    │                   │
                       │ ┌──────────────┐ │
                       │ │ File Search  │ │◀── Knowledge Base
                       │ │ Code Interp  │ │
                       │ │ Functions    │ │◀── CRM (Zendesk/SF)
                       │ └──────────────┘ │
                       └──────────────────┘
                                     │
                       ┌──────────────────┘
                       │ Vex Verification
                       │ Webhook on Run
                       └───────────────────▶ Verified Response

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate file system operations or network calls that violate sandbox policies during refund calculations or data processing
risks.mitigation: Vex scans code execution traces and blocks operations accessing unauthorized paths or external endpoints

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: RAG retrieval pulls expired pricing docs or wrong product manuals, leading to incorrect support advice
risks.mitigation: Vex validates retrieval relevance against query semantic similarity and document metadata freshness

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Long support conversations exceed 128k token context, causing incoherent responses without warning
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and triggers summarization or escalation when approaching limits

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Assistant fabricates invalid Zendesk ticket parameters (wrong customer IDs, invalid priorities), causing API failures
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validates all function call JSON against CRM API specifications before execution

implementation.title: Handle Customer Thread with Run Completion Webhook
implementation.description: Process customer messages in persistent threads and register webhook for Vex verification on run completion

implementation.title: CRM Function Calling for Ticket Creation
implementation.description: Implement required functions for Zendesk ticket creation and order lookup with proper schema validation

implementation.title: Monitor Thread Context and Trigger Escalation
implementation.description: Track token usage and escalate long conversations to human agents before context limits cause failures

vexIntegration.title: Install and Initialize Vex SDK
vexIntegration.description: Install vex-sdk and configure with your verification project for OpenAI webhook integration
vexIntegration.code:
pip install openai vex-sdk

from vex import VexClient

vex_client = VexClient(
    api_key="vex_YOUR_API_KEY",
    project_id="cs-openai-assistants",
    base_url="https://api.vex.dev"
)

# Configure verification checks for customer support
checks = [
    "response_accuracy",
    "pii_filtering",
    "tone_consistency=professional",
    "policy_compliance=support",
    "escalation_triggers"
]

vexIntegration.title: Webhook Verification on Run Completion
vexIntegration.description: Verify assistant output in webhook handler before customer delivery with all required checks

vexIntegration.title: Dynamic Escalation Based on Vex Results
vexIntegration.description: Handle verification failures with appropriate escalation workflows for compliance violations

cta.heading: Secure Your OpenAI Assistants API Customer Support
cta.description: Deploy Vex verification in minutes to protect your customer support agents from compliance risks and ensure reliable, safe interactions.
